Daniel
Castro

Engineer who builds SaaS products end-to-end and understands the business behind them. Helping startups and companies ship faster, scale smarter, and turn ideas into real products.

View my work
scroll to explore

I'm a Software and Industrial Engineering student at Universidad de los Andes, with experience at Amazon Audible and Capital One — where I learned how world-class organizations think about metrics, retention, and performance. I build full-stack products end-to-end. What sets me apart isn't the tech stack — it's understanding the business problem behind every system I build.

What I bring
01
Systems Thinker

Designing systems end-to-end — from data models to deployment, with a focus on scalability and clean architecture.

02
Business-Aware

Building with business impact in mind — prioritizing speed, retention, and sustainable growth.

03
Full-Stack Builder

Owning the full product lifecycle — backend, frontend, and infrastructure without handoff friction.

Case Study — Live Product

Mozes

Live on Vercel

A platform that helps agencies manage clients, projects, and workflows without the usual operational friction.

The Problem

Agencies were losing too much time managing clients, projects, and workflows across scattered tools. Existing solutions forced them into fragmented processes and constant context switching. There was no efficient way to unify both flows in a single system.

Stack
Next.jsTypeScriptPostgreSQLPrismaTailwind CSSVercel
Key Decision

I chose Next.js + Prisma + PostgreSQL over more fragmented architectures because it enables fast development with a consistent, type-safe foundation. This allowed optimizing development speed and maintainability despite reduced initial decoupling.

Result

Built solo end-to-end — full authentication, client management, project tracking, and workflow system. From zero to deployment, handling every layer of the stack independently.

View live product
What I build
Mozes
SaaS for agencies to manage clients, projects, and workflows — built end-to-end with authentication, payments, and scalable architecture.
Route Optimization Platform
Web tool for solving real-world optimization problems like routing, scheduling, and resource allocation — combining mathematical models with interactive visualization.soon
